testGroups.name: Compose File Validation
testGroups.description: Validate Docker Compose YAML syntax and structure

scenarios.name: Dev compose YAML valid
scenarios.description: Development docker-compose.yml has valid YAML syntax

scenarios.name: Prod compose YAML valid
scenarios.description: Production docker-compose.prod.yml has valid YAML syntax

scenarios.name: Dev services defined
scenarios.description: All 5 required services defined: php, nginx, mysql, redis, node

scenarios.name: Prod services defined
scenarios.description: All 4 required services defined: nginx, api, mysql, redis

scenarios.name: Environment template exists
scenarios.description: .env.example file exists with documented variables

testGroups.name: Development Environment
testGroups.description: Validate development environment startup and service readiness

scenarios.name: Dev services start
scenarios.description: All development services successfully start

scenarios.name: MySQL health check
scenarios.description: MySQL service reaches healthy state and responds to ping
scenarios.command: docker-compose exec -T mysql mysqladmin ping -h localhost

scenarios.name: Redis health check
scenarios.description: Redis service responds to PING command

scenarios.name: PHP service running
scenarios.description: PHP-FPM service is running and responding

scenarios.name: Nginx service running
scenarios.description: Nginx reverse proxy service is running

scenarios.name: Node dev server running
scenarios.description: Node.js dev server is running for Vue development

scenarios.name: Network communication
scenarios.description: Services can communicate across Docker network
scenarios.command: docker-compose exec -T php php -r "echo 'PHP running on network';"

testGroups.name: Production Environment
testGroups.description: Validate production environment configuration and security

scenarios.name: Prod config validates
scenarios.description: Production compose validates with environment variables

scenarios.name: Prod services defined
scenarios.description: All production services present (4 services)

scenarios.name: Env var enforcement
scenarios.description: Missing required environment variables cause validation to fail

scenarios.name: API health endpoint
scenarios.description: API responds to health check requests

scenarios.name: Static frontend served
scenarios.description: Frontend static files served correctly in production

testGroups.name: Data Persistence
testGroups.description: Verify data survival across container restarts

scenarios.name: MySQL persistence
scenarios.description: Database data survives container restart
scenarios.command: docker-compose exec -T mysql mysql -u root -proot ksf_amortization -e "SHOW TABLES;"

scenarios.name: Redis persistence
scenarios.description: Redis data survives container restart
scenarios.command: docker-compose exec -T redis redis-cli SET test_key test_value

scenarios.name: Volume mounts functional
scenarios.description: Source code changes visible in containers

scenarios.name: PHP-MySQL connection
scenarios.description: PHP service can connect to MySQL via network

scenarios.name: PHP-Redis connection
scenarios.description: PHP service can connect to Redis via network

scenarios.name: Nginx-PHP proxying
scenarios.description: Nginx correctly proxies requests to PHP backend

scenarios.name: Nginx-Node proxying
scenarios.description: Nginx correctly proxies requests to Node dev server

scenarios.name: API response time
scenarios.description: Single API request responds within 500ms

scenarios.name: Concurrent request handling
scenarios.description: Service handles 50 concurrent requests without errors

scenarios.name: Memory usage baseline
scenarios.description: Container memory usage within expected bounds

scenarios.name: No dev files in image
scenarios.description: Production image contains no development files

scenarios.name: Non-root user
scenarios.description: Production containers run as non-root user

scenarios.name: HTTPS security headers
scenarios.description: Production serves security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options)

scenarios.name: HTTP to HTTPS redirect
scenarios.description: HTTP requests redirect to HTTPS in production

scenarios.name: Container auto-restart
scenarios.description: Containers with restart policy automatically recover

scenarios.name: Network resilience
scenarios.description: Services recover after temporary network disruption

scenarios.name: Graceful degradation
scenarios.description: Application handles service unavailability without crashing
